stem: Points A, B, C, D lie in order on a straight line. Two triangles are formed below the line: triangle ABE has vertex E off the line, and triangle CDF has vertex F off the line. Segments BE and CF cross at a point G strictly between the line and points E and F. The measure of angle ABE is 42° (on the side toward the line) and the measure of angle DCF is 70° (on the side toward the line). What is the measure, in degrees, of angle BGC?
rationale: In triangle BGC, the angle at B (∠GBC) equals ∠ABE because BE is a straight ray from B; from the description, ∠GBC = 42°. Similarly ∠GCB = 70°. The three interior angles of triangle BGC sum to 180°, so ∠BGC = 180 − 42 − 70 = 68°.
